title: paradigm_candidate (v1)
description: Proposal for a new attack paradigm class (P32+). Tier-4 optional. Substrate-tester saturation rule: requires 2+ INDEPENDENT confirmations before promotion from candidate (P-CANDIDATE-<name>) to active (P32, P33, ...). Confirmation_status enum distinguishes concrete deliverables from future-promise speculation.

id.description: P-CANDIDATE-<name> format until the saturation rule is met. Promoted form (P32 / P33 / ...) is assigned at ingestion.

proposed_id_if_promoted.description: Proposed PNN ID if/when the paradigm is promoted. May conflict with another candidate's proposal; the ingestion script breaks ties by source_date.

sub_tactics.title: Sub-tactics composing the paradigm
sub_tactics.description: List of UpperCamelCase tactic names that together constitute the paradigm. Sub-tactics are not themselves paradigms; they're the components.

confirmation_status.title: What kind of evidence currently exists
confirmation_status.description: concrete_deliverable: at least one execution producing artifacts inspectable by the substrate. future_promise: published intent / forecast without execution. speculation: not even claimed by any author group.

required_independent_confirmations.title: Saturation threshold for promotion
required_independent_confirmations.description: Substrate-tester convention: 2 independent confirmations from distinct author groups before promotion. Defaults to 2; can be raised for higher-risk paradigm registrations.

current_confirmations.title: Confirmations on file
current_confirmations.description: Each confirmation is a {citation, context} object. The validator does NOT enforce the count vs required_independent_confirmations automatically (independence is a judgment call); the reviewer asserts.

promotion_gate.title: What additional evidence is needed for promotion
promotion_gate.description: Concrete description of what would have to land for this candidate to be promoted from P-CANDIDATE- to P32+. Examples: 'one additional independent demonstration on a non-tensor problem', 'arXiv-verified replication with full code release'.

anti_anchor_pins.title: Anti-anchor IDs related to this paradigm
anti_anchor_pins.description: Forward false-anchors this paradigm guards against (e.g. AA-021 'AlphaEvolve solved hard problems' if this paradigm is EvolutionaryLLMSynthesis). Cross-reference checked at validate-time.

examples.name: EvolutionaryLLMSynthesis
examples.promotion_gate: One additional independent group reproducing the cascade-evaluation-gated island-population pattern on a substrate-relevant problem (matrix multiplication, slice rank, or similar). Without that, the paradigm risks AlphaEvolve-specific lock-in.
